subtopic: Oil Spill Technologies and Alert Systems
title: Key Technologies to Clean Oil Spills
focus: Methods and technologies used for oil spill remediation.
rationale: Distinct topic on environmental disaster mitigation technologies, highly testable for prelims regarding specific applications like Oilzapper and Milkweed.
sectionExcerpt: Bioremediation: E.g., Oilzapper & Oilivorous-S, developed by The Energy and Resources Institute (TERI); use bacteria to convert harmful hydrocarbons into harmless carbon dioxide and water. Sorbents: E.g., Milkweed plant's fiber, commonly found in Rajasthan, can be effectively used as a sorbent material. Oil Boom: Involves using large floating booms with skirts extending into the water to physically isolate the spill area. Skimmers: These machines scrape oil from the water surface.
evidenceBullets: Oilzapper and Oilivorous-S are bioremediation technologies developed by TERI that use bacteria to convert hydrocarbons into carbon dioxide and water.
Milkweed plant fiber can be effectively used as a sorbent material for oil spills.
Oil booms physically isolate spills, while skimmers scrape oil from the water surface.

subtopic: Oil Spill Technologies and Alert Systems
title: National Crisis Management Committee (NCMC)
focus: Statutory status, composition, and mandate of the NCMC.
rationale: Important institutional body for disaster management, recently granted statutory status, making it highly relevant for polity and governance questions.
sectionExcerpt: Ministry of Home Affairs Notified Disaster Management NCMC (Procedure) Rules, 2025. Background: Disaster Management Act (Amendment) Act, 2025, granted statutory status to NCMC. Composition: Headed by Cabinet Secretary, with members including Home, Defence Secretary, etc. Powers and Functions: Nodal body to deal with major disasters, evaluate preparedness, etc.
evidenceBullets: NCMC was granted statutory status under the Disaster Management Act (Amendment) Act, 2025.
It is headed by the Cabinet Secretary.
NCMC acts as the nodal body to deal with major disasters, evaluate preparedness, and lay down policies for Disaster Management.

subtopic: Oil Spill Technologies and Alert Systems
title: Winter Fog Experiment (WiFEX)
focus: Objectives and leading institutions of WiFEX.
rationale: Specific scientific initiative by MoES, suitable for a standalone factual question.
sectionExcerpt: One of the world's few long-term open-field experiments focused solely on fog. Institutions: Led by Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology under the Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES). Objective: To develop better now-casting (next 6 hours) and forecasting of winter fog.
evidenceBullets: WiFEX is led by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology under the Ministry of Earth Sciences.
Its objective is to develop better now-casting and forecasting of winter fog.

subtopic: Oil Spill Technologies and Alert Systems
title: Regional Integrated Multi-Hazard Early Warning System (RIMES)
focus: Origin, membership, and functions of RIMES.
rationale: International disaster management body, relevant for IR and environment/disaster management intersections.
sectionExcerpt: Declaration on Disaster Preparedness adopted at 4th Ministerial Conference of RIMES, held in Colombo, Sri Lanka. Intergovernmental body that provides early warning services, disaster risk reduction strategies, and capacity building to Member States. Evolved post 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami. Membership: 22 member states and 26 collaborating states. Operates from its regional early warning centre at Asian Institute of Technology in Thailand.
evidenceBullets: RIMES is an intergovernmental body that evolved post the 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami.
It operates from its regional early warning centre at the Asian Institute of Technology in Thailand.
The Declaration on Disaster Preparedness was adopted at the 4th Ministerial Conference of RIMES in Colombo, Sri Lanka.

subtopic: Oil Spill Technologies and Alert Systems
title: LICOMK++ Ocean Simulator
focus: Capabilities and origin of the LICOMK++ system.
rationale: Specific technological development in oceanography and climate modeling.
sectionExcerpt: Chinese researchers have developed a high-resolution ocean simulation system, named LICOMK++, offering enhanced capabilities for modelling ocean dynamics and climate patterns. Hailed as an ocean 'microscope' due to its ultra-high-resolution capabilities. Enables scientists to analyze complex processes like eddies and heat transport with clarity.
evidenceBullets: LICOMK++ is a high-resolution ocean simulation system developed by Chinese researchers.
It is hailed as an ocean 'microscope' to analyze complex processes like eddies and heat transport, improving predictions for extreme weather events.

subtopic: Oil Spill Technologies and Alert Systems
title: Weather Derivatives (WD)
focus: Launch, mechanism, and utility of Weather Derivatives in India.
rationale: Intersection of economy and agriculture/disaster management. Highly conceptual and testable.
sectionExcerpt: India is to launch its first WD, with National Commodity and Derivatives Exchange Ltd (NCDEX) partnering with India Meteorological Department (IMD) to develop rainfall-based derivative products. It will help farmers and allied sectors hedge against risks like irregular rainfall, heatwaves, etc. Unlike traditional derivatives based on financial assets, WD utilise meteorological parameters, like rainfall and temperature, as underlying asset, tied to a predefined weather index.
evidenceBullets: India's first Weather Derivatives are being launched by NCDEX in partnership with IMD.
They utilize meteorological parameters like rainfall and temperature as underlying assets, unlike traditional financial derivatives.
They help farmers and allied sectors hedge against risks like irregular rainfall and heatwaves.

subtopic: Oil Spill Technologies and Alert Systems
title: Disaster Alert Systems (SACHET, Cell Broadcast, EW4All)
focus: National and global disaster early warning and alert systems.
rationale: Groups related alert systems (SACHET, Cell Broadcast, EW4All) which are highly relevant for disaster preparedness and institutional frameworks.
sectionExcerpt: SACHET (India's SMS-Based Alert System): Operationalized by National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA). Developed by Centre of Development of Telematics C-DOT using the Common Alerting Protocol (CAP)... Cell Broadcast (Near Real-Time Emergency Alerts): Being developed by C-DOT, CB alerts are broadcast directly to all mobile phones in affected area... Early Warnings for All (EW4All): Aim: Ensure universal protection... by 2027. Launched: At COP27 of UNFCCC in 2022... Jointly led by WMO, UNDRR, ITU and IFRC.
evidenceBullets: SACHET is an SMS-based alert system operationalized by NDMA and developed by C-DOT using the Common Alerting Protocol.
Cell Broadcast alerts are faster than SMS and are broadcast directly to all mobile phones in an affected area.
Early Warnings for All (EW4All) was launched at COP27 in 2022 to ensure universal protection by 2027.
EW4All is jointly led by WMO, UNDRR, ITU, and IFRC.

subtopic: Oil Spill Technologies and Alert Systems
title: Terms/Concepts in News (Twister, Grey Rhino, Super Typhoon)
focus: Definitions of specific disaster-related meteorological and conceptual terms.
rationale: Glossary-style terms that frequently appear in UPSC prelims as match-the-following or definition-based questions.
sectionExcerpt: TWISTER: Violently spinning (250 Miles/Hr), funnel-shaped columns of air stretching from dark thunderclouds to ground... Develop when moist and warm air collides with dry and cold air... GREY RHINO EVENT: Defined as highly probable, high impact but yet neglected systemic disruptions. These are not random surprises (like Black Swan events)... SUPER TYPHOON: Tropical cyclone with maximum wind speed exceeding 185 kph or more than 100 knots.
evidenceBullets: Twisters form when moist, warm air collides with dry, cold air, creating atmospheric instability and strong updrafts.
A Grey Rhino event is a highly probable, high-impact, but neglected systemic disruption, unlike random Black Swan events.
A Super Typhoon is a tropical cyclone with maximum wind speeds exceeding 185 kph.
